items.title: The ACT wants 26,000 new homes built in the next five years. Here's what that looks like

children.content: The ACT government describes its land release and housing supply program as "ambitious" but "achievable". So what are these 26,000 homes going to look like, and where are they going to be built?

items.title: Aboriginal woman pepper-sprayed by prison guards during self-harm incident

children.content: The use of pepper spray on an Aboriginal woman who was self-harming after being denied access to a cultural arts program was "inappropriate and should not happen," according to the ACT's prison watchdog.

image.altText: Alexander Maconochie Centre is Canberra's only prison.

items.title: Even kids want gambling ads banned, so why won't the government do it?

children.content: The government's proposed crackdown on gambling ads doesn't go as far as most Australians want. When you run the numbers, it's a win for the betting industry.

image.altText: Hands holding a mobile phone displaying a gambling app

items.title: Several infrastructure projects put on backburner as ACT 'at peak construction'

children.content: Some ACT government infrastructure projects, including health, emergency services and sports facilities in North Gungahlin, will be deferred to save about $700 million over four years.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a navy suit jacket and glasses sits indoors speaking with his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a navy suit jacket and glasses sits indoors speaking with his hands.

items.title: Knitters find key to serenity during 'party, party, party' snow season

children.content: As the NSW ski season gets underway, Jindabyne's Snowy Stitchers Social Club finds connection and mindfulness in needles and yarn.

image.altText: Rachelle Edwards, wearing a knitted beanie and gloves, standings in front of a snow field.

items.title: Canberra man facing almost 70 sexual assault, violence charges

children.content: Police said multiple women contacted them in late 2025 and early 2026 to accuse the man of sexual assault while he was in relationships with them.

image.altText: A man with his hands cuffed behind his back is led away by a police officer.

items.title: 'Cybertongue' could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste every year

children.content: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

gallery.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

thumbnail.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

items.title: How the regime is sneaking tankers past Trump's blockade

children.content: Amid new strikes, the future of the Strait of Hormuz has again been plunged into uncertainty, but ships are finding a way through.

articles.title: Pilots 'fine' after US helicopter goes down near Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Donald Trump confirms reports of a US Apache helicopter crash near the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: An Apache helicopter flying through the sky.

articles.title: Both Israel and Iran seem to be outsmarting Trump

children.content: The US president is in deepening trouble and he does not seem to know how to get himself out of it.

image.altText: Donald has his back to the camera and Benjamin stands close smiling looking at him, other people mill in background.

gallery.altText: A black and white, shadowy images of ships in the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Iran says ticket allocation for World Cup withdrawn days before tournament

children.content: Iran's football federation says the 8 per cent quota of tickets allocated to Iran for the FIFA World Cup has been revoked, meaning "there is no possibility to distribute tickets to fans".

image.altText: Iran's national soccer team attend a farewell ceremony

items.title: Year 12 students in Melbourne caught cheating using AI

children.content: An all-boys school in Melbourne's outer east has taken action after several students were caught cheating on a Year 12 exam using AI.

image.altText: A cream wall with the words "Mazenod College" in gold writing beside a blue and black emblem.

items.title: Notre Dame university halts new nursing student enrolments for another year

children.content: The university says it will not take new students for its nursing program for another year, as it needs to "rebalance enrolments across the program within accredited program capacity".

image.altText: A sign for the University of Notre Dame at its Fremantle campus

items.title: The Big Issue celebrates three decades in Australia with special edition

children.content: The magazine has become a lifeline for its vendors and is marking its 30th year with a special edition, pouring more proceeds in the pockets of those who need it most.

gallery.altText: A group of people wearing fluorescent vests hold 30th anniversary posters with celebratory moods.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people wearing fluorescent vests hold 30th anniversary posters with celebratory moods.

items.title: We were happier during the COVID-19 pandemic. The reason is money

children.content: Australians felt a greater sense of satisfaction during the COVID-19 pandemic than they do today, according to government data, when households are grappling with the impact of inflation, rising interest rates and a reduction in real wages.

image.altText: Couple stands together, framed by bookshelves and small colourful items for sale.

items.title: Mongols fugitive taken down by dog squad after fleeing traffic stop on foot

children.content: Police have arrested fugitive Top End Mongols bikie Brandt Graham five days after the 44-year-old escaped from custody last week, sparking an "extensive manhunt".

image.altText: A shirtless man in jeans with his face blurred is surrounded by three police officers who hold his arms

items.title: Escaped devil spotted on suburban Gold Coast CCTV after week on loose

children.content: A two-year-old Tasmanian devil has been on the loose since last Tuesday when she leapt out of her enclosure at a Gold Coast wildlife park.

image.altText: Tasmanian devil found in cctv.

items.title: Doctors call for mandatory food star ratings to stop health 'crisis'

children.content: Health bodies want star ratings on all packaged foods to help shoppers make healthier choices, but the grocery industry says the voluntary system works.

image.altText: A logo with four stars and the caption Health Star Rating on a food packet.

items.title: Foord scores in game 150 as Matildas beat Mexico

children.content: The Matildas earned a 3-1 victory in a frenetic contest against a game Mexican side, as Caitlin Foord scored in her milestone match.

image.altText: Caitlin Foord smiles

items.title: Idris Elba thinks audiences won't support a black James Bond

children.content: Idris Elba has quashed rumours he was ever in the running to play James Bond, saying audiences are unlikely to come along for the ride if Bond is played by a black actor.

items.title: The criticism Marc Fennell always gets for Stuff the British Stole

children.content: Two years in the making, the Walkley Award-winning journalist is back with the third season of his globe-trotting historical mystery series. He's treating it like it's the last.

image.altText: Marc looks to the left while standing around lush greenery.

items.title: From the Kimberley to Sweden, how heartbreak helped me take a travel leap

children.content: As I approached the age of 30, I longed to travel outside of Australia, but there was always an excuse or obstacle. A chance to share my culture with the world finally got me there.

image.altText: Molly in front of some of her art

items.title: Donald Trump booed at NBA Finals as chaos ensues outside

children.content: Two people are charged over a shooting at what was advertised as the venue for a Sydney crime boss's funeral.

image.altText: a window with a hole after it was shot

items.title: Young couple told by agent that landlords would not rent to 'people like you'

children.content: Hopeful renters say negative stereotypes about young people are making it almost impossible to secure a rental on the Sunshine Coast.

items.title: ABC boss confirms ex-news director in the dark about replacement hunt

items.title: Redevelopment of Brisbane milk factory site proposed ahead of Olympics

children.content: Developer Mark Stockwell has revealed plans for an apartment tower, hotel and Olympic hub at the Parmalat milk processing site on Brisbane's central riverfront.

items.title: Family's 'worst nightmare' after purchasing home filled with defects

children.content: After a young family invests their life savings in their dream home, only to discover it is filled with defects, SA's consumer watchdog issues a public warning notice about the builder responsible and seven other traders.

items.title: Miner Nathan River Resources collapses owing at least $300m

children.content: Iron ore mining company Nathan River Resources has gone into voluntary administration owing at least $300 million in debt, unpaid wages and royalties.

items.title: 'I apologise': Tasmanian premier pledges reforms after Ogilvie legal saga

children.content: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff has apologised and pledged to strengthen transparency around the use of public funds for politicians' legal proceedings, following a minister's resignation.

items.title: Two charged over Minns donations but premier won't face legal action

children.content: Two men will be prosecuted over donations to Chris Minns's 2015 election campaign, but the NSW premier will not face any legal action himself.

image.altText: Chris Minns NSW Premier at Parramatta East Public School

items.title: Damning report finds WA prisoners subjected to 'cruel, degrading treatment'

children.content: WA prisoners are being subjected to "cruel, inhumane or degrading treatment" because the state government has allowed prisons to become "almost fundamentally broken", according to the Inspector of Custodial Services.

image.altText: Casuarina Prison barbed wire sunset

items.title: Nate thought nobody would celebrate his 10th birthday. He was wrong

items.title: 'Violence brokers' are transforming organised crime in Sydney

items.title: Judge blocks Trump's $142,000 H-1B skilled worker visa fee

children.content: The Trump administration's planned fee for the H-1B visa was announced to prevent foreign workers from taking American jobs.

items.title: The bad blood between Poland and Ukraine left over from World War II

children.content: Poland is weighing whether to strip Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of its top honour for renaming an army unit after Ukrainian nationalist insurgents who massacred Poles in World War II.

image.altText: Veterans of the Ukrainian Insurgent Army standing, one holds a German MP40.

items.title: The Taiwan crisis nobody is watching

children.content: Taipei sits at the physical centre of the global AI economy but remains shut out of the diplomatic rooms where its sovereign rights are negotiated, often by others.
